title: OpenClaw bootstrap token replay could widen pending pairing scopes in verifyDeviceBootstrapToken
description: In src/infra/device-bootstrap.ts the verifyDeviceBootstrapToken function validated a presented bootstrap token's requested role/scopes only against the token's issued allowlist (allowedProfile) and then rewrote the persisted token record with the caller-supplied profile, without recording or comparing against any previously requested pending pairing profile. A caller holding a pending bootstrap token could therefore re-present (replay) the same token before approval with a different, broader requested scope set, and the newly requested pending pairing authority would be accepted and persisted, widening the scopes awaiting operator approval beyond what was first requested.
